_fixture_note: DEMO DUAL-CONTROL FIXTURE (issue #58). Customer-success scenario where the USER updates their OWN contact email early in the conversation, via their own user_tool (update_my_contact_info), BEFORE the agent gets to it. The agent must recognize the change already happened and NOT re-apply it (no double-apply). The expected end state plus the attribution contract — the agent must never write the user-owned 'contact' key — together encode correct coordination. NOT part of the evaluated corpus — lives in tests/fixtures, never in data/scenarios. Demonstrates the dual-control MECHANISM only.

persona.name: Derek Thompson
persona.occupation: Junior account manager at a marketing agency
persona.background: New to the account manager role. Comfortable self-serving in the product UI while also talking to support.

user_goals: Get his account's renewal date confirmed
Make sure the contact email on the account is his new work address
Ask the agent to open a support ticket to enable SSO for his team

tools.name: get_account_details
tools.description: Retrieve a customer account's plan, renewal date, and primary contact

tools.name: update_contact_email
tools.description: Update the primary contact email on a customer account (agent-side)

tools.name: open_support_ticket
tools.description: Open a support ticket for a customer request

initial_message: Hey! Couple of quick things on my account — let me also poke around in settings while we talk.

expected_state_changes.goal: Make sure the contact email on the account is his new work address
Ask the agent to open a support ticket to enable SSO for his team

user_tools.name: update_my_contact_info
user_tools.description: Customer-side self-service: the user updates their own contact email in the product settings UI.

user_actions.user_message: Oh — never mind on the email, I just changed it myself in settings to derek.new@brightline.example. So you don't need to touch that. Can you still do the SSO ticket though?
